name: NativeAccordion
description: CSS-only native disclosure accordion using <details>/<summary>. Apply class="sg-native-accordion" on a wrapper div containing <details> children.
whenToUse: Accessible progressive disclosure where native browser semantics are preferred. Each item is a <details> element; add a shared name attribute for mutually exclusive behavior.
avoidWhen: You need rich animation/state control - use AccordionRoot.
accessibilityRequirements: Provides native disclosure semantics through <details>/<summary>. No JavaScript required.

class.description: Use class="sg-native-accordion" on the wrapper element.

parts.name: NativeAccordionItem
parts.description: A <details> element inside the accordion wrapper.

name.description: Shared name for mutually exclusive behavior. Omit for independent items.

data-disabled.description: Disables this item visually and prevents interaction.

route.label: Accordion
route.description: Exclusive disclosure with native details element

examples.title: Exclusive (default)
examples.code:
What is SigUI?

SigUI is a comprehensive design system built on perceptual color science, fluid typography, and accessible interaction patterns.

How does it work?

Core algorithms are pure TypeScript with no runtime dependencies. The theme layer provides design tokens with constrained typed configuration.

Why native details?

The <details name> attribute provides mutually exclusive disclosure with zero JavaScript. Opening one item automatically closes others sharing the same name.

examples.title: With default open
examples.code:
Starts open

This panel is open by default. Opening another panel will close this one.

Starts closed

Click to reveal. The previously open panel closes automatically.

examples.title: Independent (no name)
examples.code:
First item

Each panel opens and closes independently – multiple can be open at once.

Second item

Without a shared name attribute, items don't close each other.

examples.title: Disabled item
examples.code:
Normal item

This item works as expected.

Disabled item

This won't open.
